title: Sour Tender Japanese Pork Ramen
description: A sour and tender Japanese pork ramen dish featuring fennel seeds, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This paleo-friendly recipe is easy to make and delicious to eat.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the coconut aminos, rice vinegar, and fennel seeds. Stir to combine.
Add the kombu sheets and 4 cups of water to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Remove the pork from the pot and shred it into bite-sized pieces. Return the shredded pork to the pot and stir to combine.
Cook the ramen noodles according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
To assemble the ramen, place some noodles in a bowl, then add sliced pork, chopped scallions, and a ladle of the hot broth.
